task_completion.description: The 'website_analytics.csv' dataset is loaded into Power BI Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Data' pane on the right side of the Power BI interface shows the 'website_analytics' table, typically with its columns listed below it.

task_completion.description: A Table visual is added to the report canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A table grid visual is present on the main report canvas.

task_completion.description: The Table visual displays 'Source / Medium', 'Pageviews', and 'Bounce Rate'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The table on the canvas has column headers for 'Source / Medium', 'Pageviews', and 'Bounce Rate', OR the Visualizations pane shows these three fields in the 'Columns' well while the table is selected.

task_completion.description: A Slicer visual is added to the report canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A slicer visual (which may look like a list of checkboxes, a dropdown menu, or a slider) is present on the main report canvas.

task_completion.description: The Slicer visual is configured to filter by the 'Year' column.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The slicer on the canvas displays year values (2019, 2020), OR the Visualizations pane shows the 'Year' field in the 'Field' well while the slicer is selected.

task_completion.description: The report is saved as 'analytics_report.pbix' to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar of the Power BI Desktop window displays 'analytics_report - Power BI Desktop', or a 'Save As' dialog shows the file being saved to the Desktop with this name.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Power BI Desktop application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard Power BI Desktop interface elements (ribbon, canvas, visualization panes) being clicked and manipulated, rather than a script or terminal window generating the file.

integrity.description: The data shown in the visuals matches the provided synthetic dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The slicer shows years '2019' and '2020'. The table shows 'Source / Medium' values that are mostly single or double letters (e.g., A, B, AA) rather than real-world URLs.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file from within Power BI rather than just renaming an existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using the 'File > Save As' menu in Power BI Desktop to save the report to the Desktop.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 13 columns including 'Source / Medium', 'Pageviews', 'Bounce Rate', and 'Year'. The 'Year' column contains only '2019' and '2020'. The 'Source / Medium' column contains synthetic data, mostly single/double-letter source codes (A, B, C...Z, AA, BB). The task is performed on Windows 11 using Microsoft Power BI Desktop.
